model_narrative: We are planning a naval evacuation. A fixed set of candidate voyages is available, each voyage being a route that calls at one or more coastal ports, and each voyage can be run with ships of different size classes. We decide how many times to run each voyage with each ship class and how many men to carry from each port on each of those sailings. The objective is to minimize a weighted combination of the total number of sailings, the total distance sailed, and the total man-distance moved, with the number of sailings weighted most heavily and the man-distance term weighted least.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the ports where stranded men must be picked up and evacuated

sets.name: j
sets.doc: the candidate voyages; each voyage is a predefined route that calls at one or more ports

sets.name: k
sets.doc: the ship classes, ordered from smallest to largest carrying capacity

sets.name: a
sets.doc: the voyage-port assignments; a pair belongs to this set when that voyage actually calls at that port, so it tells you which ports each voyage can pick up men from

sets.name: sc
sets.doc: the port and ship-class pairs for which that class of ship is physically able to serve that port; a class missing for a port cannot dock or load there

sets.name: vc
sets.doc: the feasible voyage and ship-class combinations; a combination is feasible only when the ship class can serve every port that the voyage calls at, so only these voyage and class pairs may actually be operated

params.name: d
params.doc: the number of men that must be evacuated from each port, in men

params.name: shipcap
params.doc: the carrying capacity of a single ship of each class, in men per ship

params.name: n
params.doc: the number of ships available in each class, a hard upper limit on how many sailings of that class can be operated in total

params.name: dist
params.doc: the distance associated with each voyage, used only in the objective

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the number of times a given voyage is operated using a given ship class; a whole number since each sailing is one physical trip

vars.name: y
vars.doc: the number of men carried from a given port on a given voyage operated by a given ship class

description: Every port must have all of its stranded men picked up. For each port, the total number of men carried away from that port across all the sailings that actually call there must be at least the number of men that need to be evacuated from it.

model_narrative: We are planning a naval evacuation. A fixed set of candidate voyages is available, each voyage being a route that calls at one or more coastal ports, and each voyage can be run with ships of different size classes. We decide how many times to run each voyage with each ship class and how many men to carry from each port on each of those sailings. The objective is to minimize a weighted combination of the total number of sailings, the total distance sailed, and the total man-distance moved, with the number of sailings weighted most heavily and the man-distance term weighted least.

sets.doc: the ports where stranded men must be picked up and evacuated

vars.name: y
vars.doc: the number of men carried from a given port on a given voyage operated by a given ship class

description: A sailing can only carry as many men as the ship operating it can hold. For each feasible combination of a voyage and a ship class, the total number of men carried out of all the ports that voyage calls at must not exceed the capacity of one ship of that class multiplied by the number of times that voyage is run with that class. Combinations that are not feasible are left out.

We decide how many times to run each voyage with each ship class and how many men to carry from each port on each of those sailings. The objective is to minimize a weighted combination of the total number of sailings, the total distance sailed, and the total man-distance moved, with the number of sailings weighted most heavily and the man-distance term weighted least.

description: We cannot run more sailings of a ship class than we have ships in that class. For each ship class, the total number of sailings operated with that class, added up over all the voyages it can feasibly run, must not exceed the number of ships available in that class.

model_narrative: We are planning a naval evacuation. A fixed set of candidate voyages is available, each voyage being a route that calls at one or more coastal ports, and each voyage can be run with ships of different size classes. We decide how many times to run each voyage with each ship class and how many men to carry from each port on each of those sailings. The objective is to minimize a weighted combination of the total number of sailings, the total distance sailed, and the total man-distance moved, with the number of sailings weighted most heavily and the man-distance term weighted least.

sets.doc: the ports where stranded men must be picked up and evacuated

vars.name: y
vars.doc: the number of men carried from a given port on a given voyage operated by a given ship class

description: To build the complete model, enforce the following relationships in order. First, every port must have all of its stranded men picked up. For each port, the total number of men carried away from that port across all the sailings that actually call there must be at least the number of men that need to be evacuated from it. Second, a sailing can only carry as many men as the ship operating it can hold. For each feasible combination of a voyage and a ship class, the total number of men carried out of all the ports that voyage calls at must not exceed the capacity of one ship of that class multiplied by the number of times that voyage is run with that class. Combinations that are not feasible are left out. Finally, we cannot run more sailings of a ship class than we have ships in that class. For each ship class, the total number of sailings operated with that class, added up over all the voyages it can feasibly run, must not exceed the number of ships available in that class.
